displayTitle: Know the Weld Before You Scan

beats.narration: Every weld is a small casting, poured one bead at a time. Know how it was made, and you can predict what's hiding inside.

payload.title: KNOW THE WELD
payload.sub: A small casting, poured one bead at a time.

beats.narration: Each process leaves a fingerprint. Stick traps slag. Wire feed lays cold, unfused metal. Submerged arc cracks its centerline. TIG drops tungsten.
Cap and toes on top, root below, fusion lines where weld meets plate, and around everything, the heat-affected zone. Examine both — weld plus zone.

payload.heading: Anatomy of a finished groove weld

beats.narration: Coverage is trigonometry. At the half skip, leg one hits the root; at the full skip, leg two returns to the cap.
So here's the rule: know the weld, draw the path, and scan — don't search. Prediction turns searching into scanning.

payload.sub: Predict the flaw before the probe touches steel.
